title: Malformed numeric XML entities can abort Substack feed parsing
reasoning: decodeXml converts any finite numeric entity with String.fromCodePoint, but it does not ensure the code point is within Unicode's valid range. An entity like &#9999999999; is finite but out of range, so String.fromCodePoint throws. Because parseFeed applies stripHtml/decodeXml while mapping items, one malformed entity can abort parsing the whole feed and force fetchEssays to fall back to stale or empty snapshot data.
recommendation: Validate numeric entities with codePoint >= 0 && codePoint <= 0x10FFFF before calling String.fromCodePoint, returning the original entity for invalid values.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The Substack tests cover ordinary named entities, whitespace, read time, and a well-formed feed, but do not include invalid numeric entities or malformed feed content.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a stripHtml/decodeXml test with an out-of-range numeric entity and assert it does not throw and preserves or safely drops the invalid entity.
minimumFixScope: src/lib/data/substack.ts decodeXml guard plus one Substack parser regression test.

history.note: Implemented in working tree; native checks pass. Full Clawpatch provider revalidation was curtailed after confirming first five fixes because it was too slow.
